scenario: A custom Outbound Connector built with the SDK has a property apiUrl that should appear in the Web Modeler property panel under "Endpoint Configuration." The team is wiring up the Element Template. Which binding type in the Element Template applies the value to the Connector's internal config?
question: Which binding type in the Element Template applies the value to the Connector's internal config?

options.text: Both zeebe:input and zeebe:taskHeader are valid binding types for custom Connector properties in an Element Template. The zeebe:input binding creates an Input Mapping that surfaces the value as a local task variable accessible to the Connector function. The zeebe:taskHeader binding stores the value as a job custom header read via getCustomHeaders(). Choose based on whether the value should appear in process variables or remain in headers only.

binding: { type: "zeebe:input", name: "apiUrl" } -- creates an Input Mapping on the Service Task that injects the property value as a local variable named apiUrl available to the Connector function during execution. This binding is valid and commonly used when the Connector reads configuration via input variables.

binding: { type: "property", name: "name" } -- binds the property to the BPMN element's XML attribute named name, such as the task's display name field. This binding type writes to structural BPMN model properties, not to Connector input configuration. It cannot inject a value into the Connector's runtime configuration or make it available to the Connector function logic during job execution.

binding: { type: "zeebe:taskHeader", key: "apiUrl" } -- binds the property as a Zeebe job custom header with key apiUrl. getCustomHeaders(). This binding keeps the configuration value out of the process variable scope. It is valid for Connector configuration but on its own does not cover use cases where the value must appear as a variable.

b.text: Incorrect. property binding writes to a BPMN-element attribute (e.g., name), not a Connector config.

d.text: Incorrect. Element Templates define a binding type for each property mapping the user input to either a BPMN/Zeebe construct or a Connector input. Per template-properties.md:299-360, both zeebe:input (Input Mapping -- local task variable, lines 299-318) and zeebe:taskHeader (job header, lines 341-360) are valid binding types for Connector properties. Use zeebe:input when the Connector function reads via input mappings (variable surfaced to downstream tasks); use zeebe:taskHeader when the value is static config consumed only by the Connector function (not surfaced as a process variable). Choose based on integration style.
